title: Evangelion Superbowl I
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Superbowl I (?!)             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Mon Sep 19, 2011 9:27 pm Due to american football season starting up. If we were to organize an evangelion superbowl, who'd play what? (By which I mean which mech/Angel) My thoughts: Unit 02: non-play side inside/outside linebacker. Is there any other position? Asuka trucks. It's in her personality. Unit 01: QB: dude who gets protected by everybody and then reaps a very large share of credit. MP Evas: Linemen. All indistinguishable, hardly lauded. Their time in the camera lasts only as long as the ball is in the immediate vicinity J.A.: Linemen(assuming replication): big, heavy, presumably strong. Maybe he can't react fast enough to play line but...dem long long arms. Unit 03: linebacker/cornerback/F.S.: very mobile, and lol rubber arms=interceptions liek a boss./cornerback/F.S.: very mobile, and lol rubber arms=interceptions liek a boss. Unit 06: Wide receiver/free safety/running back: agile, fast, light, run around 'em all. Unit 05 Provisional: um...4 legs... that's all I can think of...

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Tue Sep 20, 2011 5:55 am Forget the team, who's going to be the cheerleaders?

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Tue Sep 20, 2011 6:09 am Reis. Endless spam of Reis. (Worst) Best cheer team ever. *In cold, emotionless voice* We've got the spirit... No, seriously: Misato alone.

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Tue Sep 20, 2011 6:46 am Ramiel as quarterback Zeruel as a linebacker Not too sure about the rest but Kaworu would be team angel's cheerleader lololololol

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Tue Sep 20, 2011 7:17 pm I'd rather watch an EGF superbowl.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Sep 20, 2011 7:30 pm It's threads like this that make me wish I knew anything about football.

[]:             Quote Post by John » Tue Sep 20, 2011 8:16 pm God bless Google. (But I think these are wrong) Zeruel, definitive quarterback, nothing being able to stop him. LOL at Israfel being Middle Linebacker... then Outside Linebacker See what I did there? And the MAGI giving us 8640p HDTV and live-full statistics.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Sep 21, 2011 11:23 am There's nothing wrong with that chart. The defense is a 4-3 scheme while there are also 3-4 schemes with a nose tackle and 4 line backers. Aside from the 5 on the line, you can mix and match your wide outs with ends and backs, you can have no backs if you want. Technically, you can go without a quarterback and use one of your backs to take the snap (wildcat).

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Wed Sep 21, 2011 2:13 pm Anyone think Zeurel makes an excellent left tackle? Long arms, great strength, speedy arms. @ The chart The whole D-line lining right on the O line...makes me nervous. They can't shoot gaps as well and will get destroyed, since it's 4 on 5...

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Sep 21, 2011 2:33 pm You have 4 line up in dime defenses, or even more than that as a way to defense rushing. Defending against the run, you don't want large gaps in your line. Some run blitzes don't even have any safety coverage, they just line up (stack the box).

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Wed Sep 21, 2011 8:13 pm I'm used to a run defense w/ 2 MLBs, 2 OLBs, 1 FS, 4 Linemen shading the O line (outside shade of strong tackle and strong guard, inside shade of weak guard, outside shade of weak tackle.). Each lineman fills the gap and blitzing backers fill the rest or rush through an open hole as the runner comes out of it.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Thu Sep 22, 2011 2:02 pm gendo as coach for team eva , and Keel as coach for team angel,with gendo in permanent gendo pose .

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Thu Sep 22, 2011 6:22 pm But Misato calls the plays.

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Fri May 04, 2012 9:45 am John wrote: God bless Google. (But I think these are wrong) Zeruel, definitive quarterback, nothing being able to stop him. LOL at Israfel being Middle Linebacker... then Outside Linebacker See what I did there? And the MAGI giving us 8640p HDTV and live-full statistics.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Fri May 04, 2012 11:48 am Curse my Europeanness, and hence my lack of knowledge of American football!

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Fri May 04, 2012 10:39 pm driftking18594 wrote: Curse my Europeanness, and hence my lack of knowledge of American football!

[]: Foot-ball-err... B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat May 05, 2012 12:56 am ^ Don't forget the foreward pass. Though in saying that, I've now exhausted my knowledge on the make-up of the game. That chart alone more than doubled it.
